summary: The diff implements resizable images (REQ-1), resizable text elements (REQ-2), and rotation via the Free Transform tool (REQ-3) in AttackPathCanvas.tsx and useAttackPathState.ts. The state types TextLabel and ImageElement now include width, height, and rotation fields. Resize handles (8 positions) and rotation handle logic are present in the canvas component. However, save/load serialization in AttackPathPage.tsx does not persist width, height, or rotation for textLabels, meaning those values would be lost on save/reload.

missing_requirements.requirement: Rotation is preserved when the diagram is saved and reloaded.
missing_requirements.reason: In AttackPathPage.tsx, the handleSaveConfirm function maps textLabels without including rotation, and the truncated diff does not confirm rotation is included for images either. These fields appear to be dropped during serialization to DiagramState, so rotation would not survive a save/reload cycle.

missing_requirements.requirement: Width and height are stored on the TextLabel element and persisted on save/reload.
missing_requirements.reason: While width and height fields exist on the TextLabel interface in useAttackPathState.ts, the save serialization in AttackPathPage.tsx maps textLabels using only content, position, fontSize, color, and fontWeight — omitting width and height. Dimensions would be lost on save and not restored on reload.
